name: Lefse- Norwegian Potato Pancakes
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash, peel and boil the potatoes until tender. Drain thoroughly and shake pan over low heat to dry. Mash or grind the potatoes into a smooth mixture.
2. Combine mashed potatoes with butter, milk, salt, sugar, MSG, and black pepper in a bowl.
3. Mix well until light and fluffy. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
4. Preheat a heavy skillet or griddle over low heat (do not grease).
5. Remove the chilled potato mixture from the refrigerator. Add about half of the flour and mix until smooth.
6. Gradually add more flour to form a smooth dough. Form into a ball, turn onto a lightly floured surface, and roll out to a round about 1/8 inch thick.
7. Cut the rolled dough into rounds measuring approximately 6 inches in diameter.
8. Test the skillet by dropping a few drops of water; if they sizzle and form beads, the heat is ready (do not grease).
9. Cook each lefse round until lightly browned on one side, about 2-3 minutes. Flip carefully and brown the other side for another 1-2 minutes.
10. Continue cooking the lefse rounds as needed until they are lightly browned and dry. Remove from the skillet and place on a clean, dry towel to cool completely.
11. Dust with powdered sugar before serving. Enjoy!
